items.title: CVE-2026-32647: NGINX Open Source and NGINX Plus have a vulnerability in the ngx_http_mp4_module...
items.description: NGINX Open Source and NGINX Plus have a vulnerability in the ngx_http_mp4_module module, which might allow an attacker to trigger a buffer over-read or over-write to the NGINX worker memory resulting in its termination or possibly code execution, using a specially crafted MP4 file. This issue affects NGINX Open Source and NGINX Plus if it is built with the ngx_http_mp4_module module and the mp4 di
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-32647 is a vulnerability in the ngx_http_mp4_module of NGINX Open Source and NGINX Plus, allowing an attacker to potentially execute code or crash the NGINX worker process via a specially crafted MP4 file. The real-world impact is a high risk of data breach or ransomware. The most important defender action is to patch or update NGINX to prevent exploitation of this vulnerability.

items.title: CVE-2026-30662: ConcreteCMS v9.4.7 contains a Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability in the File ...
items.description: ConcreteCMS v9.4.7 contains a Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability in the File Manager component. The 'download' method in 'concrete/controllers/backend/file.php' improperly manages memory when creating zip archives. It uses 'ZipArchive::addFromString' combined with 'file_get_contents', which loads the entire content of every selected file into PHP memory. An authenticated attacker can exploit th
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-30662 is a Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability in ConcreteCMS v9.4.7's File Manager component, allowing authenticated attackers to exploit improper memory management when creating zip archives. This can lead to network denial of service. To defend, ensure proper input validation and memory management in file handling operations.

items.title: CVE-2026-30661: iCMS v8.0.0 contains a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the User Mana...
items.description: iCMS v8.0.0 contains a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the User Management component, specifically within the index.html file. This allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary web script or HTML via the regip or loginip parameters.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-30661 is a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in iCMS v8.0.0, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary web script or HTML via the regip or loginip parameters. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 6.1, indicating a medium severity impact. To defend, ensure the User Management component is updated to the latest version.

items.description: SQL injection in Solicitante::resetaSenha() in esiclivre/esiclivre v0.2.2 and earlier allows unauthenticated remote attackers to gain unauthorized access to sensitive information via the cpfcnpj parameter in /reset/index.php
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-30655 is a SQL injection vulnerability in esiclivre/esiclivre v0.2.2 and earlier, allowing unauthenticated attackers to access sensitive information via the cpfcnpj parameter. This vulnerability is exploitable via the /reset/index.php endpoint. Defenders should update the affected application to a patched version.

items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-30653 is a remote denial-of-service vulnerability in Free5GC v.4.2.0 and earlier, exploitable via user execution (T1204) and network denial-of-service (T1498). items.title: CVE-2026-28755: NGINX Plus and NGINX Open Source have a vulnerability in the ngx_stream_ssl_modu...
items.description:
NGINX Plus and NGINX Open Source have a vulnerability in the ngx_stream_ssl_module module due to the improper handling of revoked certificates when configured with the ssl_verify_client on and ssl_ocsp on directives, allowing the TLS handshake to succeed even after an OCSP check identifies the certificate as revoked.   


Note: Software versions which have reached End of Technical Support (EoTS) a
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-28755 is a vulnerability in NGINX's ngx_stream_ssl_module module, allowing revoked certificates to pass OCSP checks. This enables attackers to exploit public-facing applications via T1592.002 (Software) and T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) techniques. Defenders should update NGINX to the latest version to mitigate this issue.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1592.002| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1190| C[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic

    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-28753: NGINX Plus and NGINX Open Source have a vulnerability in the ngx_mail_smtp_modul...
items.description: NGINX Plus and NGINX Open Source have a vulnerability in the ngx_mail_smtp_module module due to the improper handling of CRLF sequences in DNS responses. This allows an attacker-controlled DNS server to inject arbitrary headers into SMTP upstream requests, leading to potential request manipulation. Note: Software versions which have reached End of Technical Support (EoTS) are not evaluated.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-28753 is a vulnerability in NGINX's ngx_mail_smtp_module module, allowing an attacker-controlled DNS server to inject arbitrary headers into SMTP upstream requests. This can lead to potential request manipulation. Defenders should update NGINX to the latest version to mitigate this vulnerability.

items.title: CVE-2026-27784: The 32-bit implementation of NGINX Open Source has a vulnerability in the ngx_ht...
items.description: The 32-bit implementation of NGINX Open Source has a vulnerability in the ngx_http_mp4_module module, which might allow an attacker to over-read or over-write NGINX worker memory resulting in its termination, using a specially crafted MP4 file. The issue only affects 32-bit NGINX Open Source if it is built with the ngx_http_mp4_module module and the mp4 directive is used in the configuration file.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-27784 is a vulnerability in 32-bit NGINX Open Source's ngx_http_mp4_module module, allowing an attacker to over-read or over-write worker memory. This issue affects 32-bit NGINX Open Source if built with the module and the mp4 directive is used. Defenders should update to 64-bit NGINX or patch the 32-bit version.

This issue affects NGINX Open Source and NGINX Plus when the configuratio
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-27654 is a buffer overflow vulnerability in NGINX's ngx_http_dav_module module, allowing attackers to terminate the NGINX worker process or modify file names. items.title: CVE-2026-27651: When the ngx_mail_auth_http_module module is enabled on NGINX Plus or NGINX Open...
items.description: When the ngx_mail_auth_http_module module is enabled on NGINX Plus or NGINX Open Source, undisclosed requests can cause worker processes to terminate. This issue may occur when (1) CRAM-MD5 or APOP authentication is enabled, and (2) the authentication server permits retry by returning the Auth-Wait response header. Note: Software versions which have reached End of Technical Support (EoTS) are not
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-27651 is a remote code execution vulnerability in NGINX when the ngx_mail_auth_http_module module is enabled, allowing undisclosed requests to terminate worker processes. This issue can occur when CRAM-MD5 or APOP authentication is enabled and the authentication server permits retry. Defenders should update NGINX to the latest version to prevent exploitation.

items.title: Evolution of Ransomware: Multi-Extortion Ransomware Attacks
items.description: Multi-extortion ransomware relies on stolen data to pressure victims with public leaks. Penta Security explains how its D.AMO platform keeps exfiltrated files encrypted and useless to attackers. [...]

items.title: Massachusetts emergency communications system impacted by cyberattack
items.description: An emergency communications system used across a handful of small towns in northern Massachusetts was impacted by a cyberattack.
items.ai_summary: A cyberattack impacted the Massachusetts emergency communications system, compromising its functionality. The attack's TTPs remain unknown, but its impact on public safety is significant. Defenders should prioritize monitoring for similar attacks on critical infrastructure.

items.title: Ukraine warns Russian hackers are revisiting past breaches to prepare new attacks
items.description: In a new report, CERT-UA said attackers are revisiting previously breached infrastructure to check whether access is still available, whether exploited vulnerabilities have been patched and whether previously obtained credentials remain valid.
items.ai_summary: Russian hackers are revisiting past breaches to prepare new attacks, checking for still-accessible infrastructure, patched vulnerabilities, and valid credentials. This tactic allows them to maintain a foothold and potentially launch future attacks. Defenders should prioritize monitoring for signs of re-visited breaches and patching vulnerabilities to prevent re-exploitation.

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 006 - CERT-EU Confirms European Commission Cloud Breach, Sportradar Details Emerge, and Mandiant Quantifies Campaign at 1,000+ SaaS Environments, (Fri, Apr 3rd)
items.description: This is the sixth update to the TeamPCP supply chain campaign threat intelligence report,&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;"When the Security Scanner Became the Weapon"&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;(v3.0, March 25, 2026).&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;Update 005&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;covered developments through April 1, including the first confirmed victim disclosure (Mercor AI), Wiz&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s post-compromise cloud enumeration findings, DPRK attribution of the axios compromise, and LiteLLM&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s release resumption after Mandiant&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s forensic audit. This update covers intelligence from April 1 through April 3, 2026.
items.ai_summary: The TeamPCP supply chain campaign involves threat actors compromising email accounts (T1586.002) and exploiting supply chain vulnerabilities (T1195) to gain initial access. They then use application layer protocol manipulation (T1071) to execute malicious activities, resulting in a significant breach of the European Commission's cloud infrastructure and potentially impacting 1,000+ SaaS environments. Defenders should prioritize monitoring email accounts and supply chain activity for suspicious behavior.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    C -->|T1586.002| D[Email Accounts]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Execution]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Why Third-Party Risk Is the Biggest Gap in Your Clients' Security Posture
items.description: The next major breach hitting your clients probably won't come from inside their walls. It'll come through a vendor they trust, a SaaS tool their finance team signed up for, or a subcontractor nobody in IT knows about. That's the new attack surface, and most organizations are underprepared for it. Cynomi's new guide, Securing the Modern Perimeter: The Rise of Third-Party

items.title: French Senate passes bill that would ban children under 15 from social media
items.description: If the French effort becomes law, it would make France the first European country to follow Australia’s lead by banning social media for young teenagers.

items.title: WhatsApp Alerts 200 Users After Fake iOS App Installed Spyware; Italian Firm Faces Action
items.description: Meta-owned messaging platform WhatsApp said it alerted about 200 users who were tricked into installing a bogus version of its iOS app that was infected with spyware. According to reports from Italian newspaper La Repubblica and news agency ANSA, the vast majority of the targets are located in Italy. It's assessed that the threat actors behind the activity used social engineering

items.title: Application Control Bypass for Data Exfiltration, (Tue, Mar 31st)
items.description: In case of a cyber incident, most organizations fear more of data loss (via exfiltration) than regular data encryption because they have a good backup policy in place. items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
items.description: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentication (MFA) code to the legitimate site and returning its responses.

items.title: Kimwolf Botnet Swamps Anonymity Network I2P
items.description: For the past week, the massive "Internet of Things" (IoT) botnet known as Kimwolf has been disrupting the The Invisible Internet Project (I2P), a decentralized, encrypted communications network designed to anonymize and secure online communications. I2P users started reporting disruptions in the network around the same time the Kimwolf botmasters began relying on it to evade takedown attempts against the botnet's control servers.
